name: Eartha Kitt
abstract: Eartha Mae Kitt (née Keith; January 17, 1927 – December 25, 2008) was an American singer, songwriter, and actress. She was known for her highly distinctive singing style and her 1953 recordings of "C'est si bon" and the Christmas novelty song "Santa Baby".

works.t: Threepenny Opera: Act I. Barbara Song

about_quotes.speaker: Candido Camero
about_quotes.evidence: Well, I have worked with her several times. And she has always liked to have a conga player in her rhythm section.
about_quotes.doc_title: Candido Camero — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Donn Andre
about_quotes.evidence: what was on just the regular pop radio were things like Eartha Kitt
about_quotes.doc_title: Donn Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, June 6, 2009

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: Eartha used to come into Birdland when I used to work opposite Basie and Sarah Vaughan... She came in with the music for an 84 piece orchestra... I had Herb Ellis... play rubato... And she could sing. And she loved me for that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Eartha Kitt as being in the show 'Blue Holidays' which had a good band, but the interviewee (Joe Thomas) does not speak about her or confirm any personal interaction.

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Locke
about_quotes.evidence: That’s where Eartha Kitt came from, Robert Clary. He was called Leonard Sillman. He would get all these young people, he would find these “New Faces.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Locke interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York, NY, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: A young girl dancer in Catherine Dunham's show... was making her debut as a singer in the Perroquet Club. Her name was Eartha Kitt.
